ggvolc 0.3.0

Compute -log10(p) with infinite values capped

Source: R/utils.R
neglog10_cap.Rd

-log10() of a p-value of exactly 0 (which DESeq2 and edgeR can emit through floating-point underflow for the strongest genes) is Inf, and ggplot2 silently drops non-finite values. That would make the most significant genes disappear from the plot. This helper replaces the infinite values with a finite ceiling so those genes stay on the plot, near the top.

Usage

neglog10_cap(p, ceiling = NULL)

Arguments

p

Numeric vector of p-values.

ceiling

Optional finite value used for capping. When NULL it is set to 1.05 times the largest finite -log10(p). Pass an explicit value to keep two datasets (e.g. background and highlighted genes) on the same scale.

Value

A list with value (the capped -log10(p) vector), ceiling (the cap used), and n_capped (how many values were replaced).

On this page
